name: Pi-hole
author: InSpectre Community
description: Integrates with Pi-hole v6 REST API to pull known clients (device inventory with hostnames), DHCP leases, and recent DNS query logs for traffic enrichment and suspicious domain alerting.

config_schema.label: Pi-hole Host
config_schema.help: Base URL of your Pi-hole web interface, e.g. http://192.168.0.2 or http://pi.hole. Do not include a trailing slash.

config_schema.label: API Password
config_schema.help: Your Pi-hole web password. Used to obtain a session token via POST /api/auth. Found under Settings > Web Interface in the Pi-hole UI.

config_schema.label: Query Log Entries per Poll
config_schema.help: How many recent DNS query log entries to fetch each poll cycle. Higher values give richer traffic data but increase load. Recommended: 200–1000.

config_schema.label: Suspicious Domain Keywords
config_schema.default:
malware
phishing
tracker
botnet
cnc
c2
exploit
dynamic-dns
no-ip
duckdns
ddns
config_schema.help: One keyword per line. Any DNS query whose domain contains one of these strings will trigger a vuln.found event alert tagged 'suspicious-dns'. Case-insensitive substring match.

config_schema.label: Verify SSL Certificate
config_schema.help: Disable if your Pi-hole uses a self-signed HTTPS certificate.

config_schema.label: Block Group ID
config_schema.help: Pi-hole group ID to assign blocked clients to. Create a dedicated group in Pi-hole (Settings → Groups) with a blocklist that blocks all traffic, then enter its numeric ID here. Default: 1.

login.description: Authenticate with Pi-hole and obtain a session SID token.

get_clients.description: Fetch all known clients from Pi-hole for device inventory and hostname resolution.

get_dhcp_leases.description: Fetch active DHCP leases from Pi-hole. Only available when Pi-hole's built-in DHCP server is enabled.

get_query_log.description: Fetch recent DNS query log for traffic analysis and suspicious domain detection.

test_connection.description: Verify connectivity and credentials by checking the Pi-hole DNS blocking status.

block_client.description: Block a client by assigning it to the configured block group in Pi-hole. Requires a group whose ID matches block_group_id to exist in Pi-hole with an appropriate blocklist.

unblock_client.description: Unblock a client by returning it to Pi-hole's default group (group 0).
